content.claim: Hungary's selective secondary tracks (6- and 8-year gimnaziums) serve only 2-3% of the student population, concentrated in urban and county-seat locations; approximately 85% of Hungarian students attend schools with academic selection capacity, compared to 43% OECD average.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Figure of 85% selective-capacity schools is cited from unspecified comparison data
The 2-3% figure reflects current snapshot but lacks historical trend data
Does not account for variation in selection stringency across the 85% of schools with selection capacity
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
